intro: Benstonea poronaliva, also known as the Papuan snake palm, is a plant species native to Papua New Guinea, belonging to the family Pandanaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are associated with its health benefits, such as wound healing, fever reduction, and digestive system relief. The bioactive constituents of Benstonea poronaliva include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Benstonea poronaliva include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
